warnings.message: Some dense PDF word windows are missing from the EPUB and may indicate sliced sentence interiors
Some list or roman markers appear embedded in prose instead of starting their own paragraph
Some roman numeral headings appear in body paragraphs instead of centered heading elements
Some chapter headings are long enough to suggest swallowed body text
Repeated word windows may indicate ghost-layer duplication
A significant portion of Latin words in the EPUB are not wrapped in language spans
Some dense Latin passages from the PDF are missing from the EPUB
Some tagged Latin phrases in the EPUB do not have matching modern translations in translation_db.py

short_fragments.text: We may a little explain it: —

inline_structural_candidates.text: Lastly, the concluding chapter is devoted to an explanation of the passages in Paul and James which are alleged to be at variance but which are proved to be in perfect harmony, 20. — Ed.

All men in those days were either kept in bondage under endless fears and anxieties of mind upon the convictions of sin, or sent for relief unto indulgences, priestly pardons, penances, pilgrimages, works satisfactory of their own, and supererogatory of others

What it is that, when a justified person is guilty of sin (as guilty he is more or less every day), and his conscience is pressed with a sense thereof, as that only thing which can endanger or intercept his justified estate, his favor with God, and title unto

A very few words will also free our inquiry from any concernment in that which is called sentential justification, at the day of judgment; for of what nature soever it be, the person concerning whom that sentence is pronounced was, — (1.) Actually and complete

In opposition hereunto, the state and prayer of the publican, under the same design of seeking justification before God, are expressed. And the outward acts of his person are mentioned, as representing and expressive of the inward frame of his mind: "He stood

This, in the matter of our justification, he calls, — (1.) Χάρισμα , with respect unto the free, gratuitous grant of it by the grace of God, Δωρεὰ τῆς χάριτος , and (2.) Δώρημα , with respect unto us who receive it, — a free gift it is unto us; and (3.) Δικαίω

roman_heading_candidates.text: III. There is a justification of convinced sinners on their believing.

overlong_heading_candidates.text: IMPUTATION OF THE SINS OF THE CHURCH UNTO CHRIST — GROUNDS OF IT — THE NATURE OF HIS SURETISHIP — CAUSES OF THE NEW COVENANT — CHRIST AND THE CHURCH ONE MYSTICAL PERSON — CONSEQUENTS THEREOF

THE NATURE OF JUSTIFICATION AS DECLARED IN THE EPISTLES OF ST. PAUL, IN THAT UNTO THE ROMANS ESPECIALLY. — 3:4CHAP. 3,4,5,10; 1 CORINTHIANS 1:30; 2 Corinthians 5:212 CORINTHIANS 5:21; GALATIANS 2:16; EPHESIANS 2:8-10; PHILIPPIANS 3:8,9.)
